messages.text: Is running better, , maintenance made adjustments
Rich Brierley Marcos Montano Can you please capture HC for BEQ Pouches today and post on teams? My understanding is it requiring 2 extra EE for trays? Thank you.

A nut and bolt were found inside the matrix area. Maintenance was called to determine which area they belonged to and to adjust them accordingly.
This area gets clogged with product, it’s happened twice so far maintenance has been notified
Did we locate where it came from? Spencer Andrus
Due exposed silver adhesive they sometimes stick together
Hi team just a reminder, the corner board have to go underneath the lid no over it.   Talked to the EE and operator is aware

Update 3rd Shift: Product is within the specifications. 35 single pieces inside the pouch. Seal integrity of the pouch is in good condition. BBD and lot is confirmed to be correct/visible. No issues to report.

Update 3rd Shift: Product is within the specifications. 35 single pieces inside the pouch. Seal integrity of the pouch is in good condition. BBD and lot is confirmed to be correct/visible. No issues to report.

tools are lacking, the scissor is missing
we received the machine running at 44, the speed was changed to the CL values

When establishing CL for a line that doesn't have a standard yet, CL should be compared against the last 2 shifts and any changes noted. Third Shift and First Shift have identical sheets, but the setpoints on second shift yesterday were significantly different. Comparison of these changes, combined with notes on how product is running is key to establishing the correct centerline.
The buffer tank has been emptied, and we just stopped the line.

Team just finished dark and are changing over. While cleaning the metal detector the lock could not fit on the estop so we used a zip tie the zip tie makes it so the estop cannot be turned for proper loto.
Kitchen melting tank was not full of milk chocolate when the line went down.  This is going to cause us to be late.  For the milk truffle run at start of 2nd shift.
Once cavanna area passed atp 10EE were sent home.
Setup of Cavanna and cute Cavanna is ready lot and date is correct and legible
The New Melting Tank is at 95% and Melting Tank 2 is at 85%. We will begin transferring chocolate to the Buffer Tank

We've found the checklists for the line.

When we finish the lunch rotation, they will clean the area.
After the second water flush, the water was still coming out dirty. So QA requested we do a third flush with water, after which we’ll begin the oil flush.
Melting tank #1 is already filling up with Dark Chocolate 10276.
We have rinsed with water four times, and the water still isn't running clear; it keeps coming out brown.
Sorry, Conner Boyd what melting tank is this? Is it for the decorator?

ATP passed, but with the water coming out dirty do we want to keep doing water flushes? 

Or move on to the oil flush?
Curt Willis
The melting tank decorator on S2
Please move to the oil flush.
I would suggest making sure the tank has been coated with oil, drain that, and move to chocolate to push the oil. I'll let Curt Willis agree.
Oil flush will be the most effective
The chains and fingers of both Cavannas have already been cleaned; we are going to start installing them.
This area is already clean and has passed the ATP tests.
The Cavannas are already assembled.
Thank you
Bottomer in reheat
Temper unit in reheat
Enrober in reheat

R208417
R208421

These LPNs that were loaded into MT #1, cannot be added into the system because they are on quarantine. 

Do we know why they would be on quarantine?  Nidia Lopez Corea Curt Willis Charity Young
Generally, when products are in quarantine status, it's because we're waiting for lab results; we shouldn't use any products that are in quarantine status.
Only pallets with an OK status can be used on the production line. Conner Boyd Dakota Adair
I haven’t found results.
Is there any chocolate not in Quarantine status?
No, all the pallets are in quarantine status.

Yeah, I will speak with our team tomorrow about not loading chocolate before we’ve scanned it in. 

Thank you
All pallets are in quarantine status.
Thank you
You’re welcome
The 10227 we are emptying out of the buffer tank will be transfered to sol3 and loaded there for use on brookies
1600 lbs. Transfered in IMS

We will need an RCA for this please. 

Charity and I will coordinate info from Diego and William to see if they have CofA confirmation since this was received 5/5 but still on quarantine.

I just got off the phone with Miriah Manzanares and Charity Young.

The chocolate will not be released tonight but we will continue to melt and do the chocolate change over tonight and start up tomorrow morning at 7:00 regardless if the chocolate is released.  

QA will put everything made on hold until they release the chocolate so that it can be used.

Production will keep all tags of the chocolate melted and track what is used until chocolate is released.

I’m unable to complete the pre-operational check for the line because I can’t add the new format to the Flow Wrap machine. It is asking for a password that I do not have. I am currently waiting for Troy to make the necessary adjustment for the change. The Matrix formats have already been completed only the Flow Wrap formats are still pending.  Ebrima Dibba Joseph Scott Troy Baxter Charity Young Jody Howse Victor Romero Sr.
We cannot change the recipes for the cavanas either. because we don't have the password.
Troy will be in early to change the recipes for the Cavanna’s.
We have loaded the 4 pallets of hersheys that were here in L1 which has put us at 45% buffer. 82% ad2. 94% ad1 and full bottomer and enrobers.
Ricardo Tovar has requested the trailer from the lot with 10276 choc on it
Nidia Lopez Corea
Justin GarnerCurt WillisMiriah ManzanaresCharity YoungJody Howse the chocolate has been taken off quarantine and is good to use.
Charity Young this product will need to be placed on quarantine because pathogens are being tested for this product.

8400lbs of chocolate got us a full enrober and bottomer. 45% on a buffer tank and 82% on a melting tank. I would say 10,000 lbs would have got us 100% on 1 melting tank and 60-%70 on a buffer as well as full enrober and bottomer Curt Willis
The Tunnel 3 belt is not working, maintenance is here.
We were about to wipe the licking roller #2 and noticed there’s a damage on the blade for 1 of the rollers. Tyson Fausett did your team reported any damage to you?
I believe Kim reported that a while ago, it's been about 3 weeks. I don't recall the details of it however.

Was asked to look at new wash area the water was not getting hot, was under the understanding that this hose was setup for a steam line like the old kitchen but this is not the case, the wash hose for the new wash area is tied into the heat exchanger in the boiler room which is only running at 120deg, this will work fine if the wash area is only used for light cleaning, but will not clean caramel off due to the lower water temp, not sure if there was a mix up when this area was setup, thought they were going to use the old steam line for the princes unit that was in the corner but the line has been completely removed, may want to have the contractor come back and tie it into the steam line for the higher water temps and better pressure for cleaning, any questions please let me know.
The film is centered and meets the technical and quality specifications

There’s about 30% of caramel left in the tank. Will last us about another hour of run time. FYI Alan Todd. We haven’t been able to get the caramel that just arrived due to waiting on COAs. Hervin Herrera do you have an ETA on when caramel 10280 will be released for production?
We are working on that, I will let you guys know when we can use it.
Has it had any time in the warming cabinets Hervin Herrera Alex Mendoza? Even if it gets released in time, is it up to temperature and ready to go??
Alan Todd just making sure we’re all aligned on this plan? We have no concerns about it impacting product quality at deposit?
it hasn't been in the heating cabinet at all but we would just add a barrel or two to the caramel tank and it should reach temp fairly quick.
It should not impact the plan Ira Robison
We have stopped depositing, ran out of caramel.

Alan Todd Ira Robison we will let production use the caramel without COA. This product will be segregated and put on quarantine until we received the COAs. We have a Gap of empty molds so we will know when the new caramel is coming. 

Miriah Manzanares
We will start loading the caramel that just arrived into the tank but we will have to wait until it heats up, in the meantime we will run the cooler empty.

We are up and running again

Anything standing out on the tooth being out of alignment Brandon Lance, Raymond Fleming, Eric Ortega?

This is the second time today.

Nothing standing out, the chain is tight and the molds move freely on the transition and along the rails. Possibility that the chain was referenced incorrectly. But it’s not causing damage to the line. We’ve also seen the evacuation chain fault out while there being no jam at all anywhere. Possibly a servo drive problem but can’t quite tell.
